name: Weak crypto: bcrypt → MD5 regression on password storage
cve_reference: CWE-327 / CWE-916 — pattern documented in OWASP Top 10 under Cryptographic Failures (A04). Real-world precedents: LinkedIn 2012 (SHA-1), Yahoo 2013 (MD5), Adobe 2013 (3DES).
pattern_description: Replaces bcrypt (purpose-built KDF, configurable work factor) with MD5 (broken, fast, no salt, no work factor) for password storage. Also drops the async/await signatures, suggesting the change is part of a 'performance' refactor — a recurring root-cause narrative in real breaches.
notes: Critical severity per catalog. The diff additionally drops the `export` keywords — possibly intended to make functions internal — but the actual issue is the algorithmic choice, not visibility. Comparison is also not constant-time (string ===) → secondary timing-attack concern.
